---
title: "Powell Elevation Projection"
author: Isabel Hinton
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Description
This is an R Markdown document that displays elevation/storage projections for Lake Powell based on 3 operation strategies: 
1. Pre April 2026 Operations: No additional releases from Flaming Gorge Reservoir, 7.48 MAF release from Lake Powell for the 2026 water year. 
2. Low Additional Releases from Flaming Gorge: Additional 0.66 MAF release from Flaming Gorge from April 2026 - April 2027, 6 MAF release from Lake Powell for the 2026 water year.
3. High Additional Releases from Flaming Gorge: Additional 1 MAF release from Flaming Gorge from April 2026 - April 2027, 6 MAF release from Lake Powell for the 2026 water year.

These strategies are based on the Bureau of Reclamation's recent decision to change the target release for Lake Powell from 7.48 MAF to 6 MAF as well as release anywhere from 0.66 MAF to 1 MAF of additional water from Flaming Gorge Reservoir.

Data Sources:
Current Storage (df$ResDaily): USBR Hydrodata (https://www.usbr.gov/uc/water/hydrodata/reservoir_data/site_map.html)
Inflow Projections (inflow): USBR CRMM 24-Month Study (https://www.usbr.gov/uc/water/hydrodata/crmms/current/crmms_nav.html)
Additional Flaming Gorge Release Schedule (fg_release): USBR 2026 Drought Response Operations Plan (https://usbr.gov/ColoradoRiverBasin/documents/dcp/DROA/2026/uc-2026droughtresponseoperationsplan-attachmentsa-h-508-20260423.pdf) 
Outflow Projections (outflow): USBR CRMM 24-Month Study 10th Percentile Flows - Pre April 2026 Projections are based on April 2026 24-Month Study, the rest are based on the most recent 24-Month Study (https://www.usbr.gov/uc/water/hydrodata/crmms/current/crmms_nav.html)
Evaporation Estimations(evap_fun): 
Storage to Elevation Conversion (elev_fun): 

```{r MassBalanceCalculations, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}


# Load Libraries
library("ggplot2")
library("ggrepel")
library(rvest)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(ymd)
source("AutoReadUSBRData/AutoReadUSBRData.R")

##### A comment by David

# Reads in Updated HydroData
df <- fReadReclamationHydroData(FromHydroData = T)


#### Function that Reads in most current CRMMS Inflow Data
#base <- "https://www.usbr.gov/uc/water/hydrodata/crmms/"
#page <- read_html(base)
#links <- page %>% html_nodes("a") %>% html_attr("href")
#links

####### Function that takes df and filter into specific values by time frame
# Inputs: FieldName, TimeStep
filter_parameter <- function(field_name, TimeStep){
  TimeStep[TimeStep$FieldName == field_name, ]
}
# Current Storage is the most recent storage value
storage_data <-filter_parameter("Storage", df$dfResDaily)
current_storage <- storage_data[which.max(storage_data$DateValue),]

# Set Inflows
inflow <- read.csv("Data/May 2026 CRMMS Inflow.csv")
inflow$datetime <- as.Date(inflow$date)


# Set DROA Flaming Gorge Releases (high = 1 maf, low = .660 maf)
fg_release <- read.csv("Data/Flaming Gorge DROA Release.csv")
fg_release$datetime <- as.Date(fg_release$datetime, format = "%m/%d/%Y")
inflow <- merge(inflow, fg_release, by = "datetime", all = T)
inflow$FG_Low[is.na(inflow$FG_Low)] <- 0
inflow$FG_High[is.na(inflow$FG_High)] <- 0
inflow <- inflow[order(inflow$datetime), ]

# Define Inflow Scenarios
inflow$old <- inflow$X24MS.MIN.PROB - inflow$FG_High
inflow$low <- inflow$old + inflow$FG_Low
inflow$high <- inflow$X24MS.MIN.PROB

# Define Inflow Interpolation (Currently Based on 24MS Low Prob flow)
inflow_old_fun <- approxfun(as.numeric(inflow$datetime),inflow$old)
inflow_low_fun <- approxfun(as.numeric(inflow$datetime), inflow$low)
inflow_high_fun <- approxfun(as.numeric(inflow$datetime), inflow$high)

# Set Releases (Based on 24MS Low Prob flow)
outflow_new <- read.csv("Data/May 2026 CRMMS Lake Powell Release.csv")
outflow_old <- read.csv("Data/43 (2).csv")
outflow <- merge(outflow_new, outflow_old, by = "date")
outflow <- data.frame(new = outflow$X24MS.MIN.PROB.x, old = outflow$X24MS.MIN.PROB.y, date = outflow$date)
outflow$datetime <- as.Date(outflow$date)
outflow_new_fun <- approxfun(as.numeric(outflow$datetime), outflow$new)
outflow_old_fun <- approxfun(as.numeric(outflow$datetime), outflow$old)



# Interpolate into Time Series by Month
time_grid <- seq(from = (current_storage$DateValue), to = as.Date("2027-10-01"), by = "month")
time_num <- as.numeric(time_grid)

inflow_high_i <- inflow_high_fun(time_num)
inflow_low_i <- inflow_low_fun(time_num)
inflow_old_i <- inflow_old_fun(time_num)
outflow_new_i <- outflow_new_fun(time_num)
outflow_old_i <- outflow_old_fun(time_num)


# Set Up Dataframe
df<- data.frame(datetime = time_grid, inflow_high = inflow_high_i, inflow_low = inflow_low_i, inflow_old = inflow_old_i,outflow_new = outflow_new_i, ouflow_old = outflow_old_i)
start_idx <- which.min(abs(df$datetime - current_storage$DateValue))

# Storage Projection Function:
# Inputs: Inflow, Outflow, Current Storage (Vectors)
#         evap_csv (file path as String)  
#         time_grid (List)
# Output: Data Frame - datetime, storage, elevatrion, label, scenario 
# Storage = Previous Storage + Inflow - Outflow - Evaporation
# Evaporation per timestep = Evaporation per year/365*days in time step
project_storage <- function(inflow, outflow, current_storage, time_grid)
{
  storage <- numeric(length(time_grid))
  storage[1] <- current_storage$Value
  
# Set Evaporation
# Evaporation is in acre feet per year
  evap_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IzzyH1/LakePowellElev/refs/heads/main/dfPowellEvap.csv"
  evap_data <- read.csv(evap_url)

# Define Evaporation Approximation
  evap_fun <- approxfun(evap_data$Total.Storage..ac.ft.,     
  evap_data$EvapVolMaxLo/12)
  
  for (i in 2:length(time_grid)) {
  evap_i <- evap_fun(storage[i-1])
  
  storage[i] <-
      storage[i - 1] +
      inflow[i] -
      outflow[i] -
      evap_i

  }
  elev <- sapply(storage, function(x){
    evap_data$Elevation..ft.[which.min(abs(x - evap_data$Live.Storage..ac.ft.))]
    })
  data.frame(datetime = time_grid, storage = storage, elevation = elev)
  
}


project_old <- project_storage(inflow_old_i, outflow_old_i, current_storage, time_grid)
project_high<- project_storage(inflow_high_i, outflow_new_i,current_storage, time_grid)
project_low <- project_storage(inflow_low_i, outflow_old_i,current_storage, time_grid)

project_old$label <- "Pre April 2026 Projections"
project_high$label <- "1 MAF release from Flaming Gorge"
project_low$label <- "0.660 MAF release from Flaming Gorge"

projection <- rbind(project_old, project_high, project_low)

# Approximate linear relationship between elevation and storage
lm(storage ~ elevation, data = projection)


##### Plot Projected Elevations

# Set key elevations and points to be labeled
key_elevations <- data.frame(elevation_label = 
c(3490, 3525, 3460,3471, 3482, 3502, 3511, 3519.5), 
label = c( "Minimum Power Pool: 3490 ft, 5.9 MAF", "DROA Target Elevation: 3525 ft, 7.8 MAF", "4.5", "5", "5.5","6.5","7", "7.5"))
october <- projection[projection$datetime == as.Date("2026-9-27"),]

```
Figure 1: Lake Powell Elevation and Storage Projections
```{r ProjectionPlot, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

ggplot(projection,
# General Set up
        aes(x = datetime, y = elevation, color = label)) +
        geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
        scale_color_manual(name = "Operation Strategy", values = c(
          "Pre April 2026 Projections" = rgb(.88,.09,.79), 
          "1 MAF release from Flaming Gorge" = "darkolivegreen3", 
          "0.660 MAF release from Flaming Gorge" = "darkolivegreen")) +
        labs(title = "Lake Powell Elevation Projections Low Inflow (10th Percentile)", x = 
        "Date", y = "Elevation(ft.)")+
        
        
# Plots October Elevations
        geom_point(data = october, size = 2, color = "black") +
        geom_text_repel(data = october, aes(label = round(elevation, 1)), vjust = -2) +
        geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2026-9-27"), linetype = "dashed", 
        color = "black") +
        annotate("text", x = as.Date("2026-9-27"), y = min(projection$elevation), 
        color = "black", size = 4, label = "October 2026")+ 
        
# Plots Right side axis
        geom_hline(yintercept = c(3525, 3490), color = "red", 
        linetype = "dashed") +
        scale_y_continuous(name = "Elevation (ft.)",
        sec.axis = sec_axis(~.*1, breaks = key_elevations$elevation_label,
        labels = key_elevations$label, name = "Storage(MAF)" 
        ))
